 {"id":"br-2tnl.5","title":"[track] Hybrid Fusion, Two-Tier Refinement, and Reranking","description":"## Purpose
Implement hybrid search quality stack: lexical + semantic candidate fusion, progressive two-tier refinement, and reranking.

## Scope
- Candidate generation orchestration.
- RRF fusion with deterministic tie-breakers.
- Two-tier fast→quality refinement path.
- Cross-encoder reranking and score blending.
- Explainable score decomposition and cache/warmup behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Candidate orchestration + RRF fusion + two-tier refinement are implemented.
- Optional reranker integration works with fallback behavior.
- Hybrid responses include merged explainability metadata and cache/warmup controls.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"track","assignee":"CloudyTiger","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.533469337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:26.068774755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hybrid","quality","reranking","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.533469337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:35.240065196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.432717942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.633790517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":233,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 5 reasoning:

- Hybrid quality gains come from principled fusion (RRF) and progressive refinement, not from ad-hoc score mixing.
- Two-tier search is specifically chosen to protect latency while still allowing high-quality refinement.
- Reranker is optional-at-runtime but mandatory-in-design for top-end precision.
- Explainability is required so operators can trust why a result ranked highly, especially when multiple scoring stages are combined.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:39:13Z"},{"id":690,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","author":"AzureBarn","text":"Coordination update: triaged with bv --robot-triage/--robot-plan and claimed this track (status=in_progress). Active child owners detected: br-2tnl.5.1 (IvoryMeadow), br-2tnl.5.10.2 (AzureHarbor). I am selecting an unblocked child next to avoid overlap and will post artifacts + diagnostics in-thread.","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10","title":"Two-Tier Search Hardening: Thread-Safe Init, Embedder Fallbacks, Progressive Refinement","description":"## Background

Code review of the two-tier search implementation identified three areas for hardening:

1. **Lazy initialization race condition** - `try_two_tier_search` may create duplicate `TwoTierBridge` instances when called concurrently before initialization completes.

2. **Zero-filled quality embeddings** - When quality embedder is unavailable, documents get zero-filled quality embeddings which provide no meaningful similarity signal.

3. **Progressive refinement not implemented** - The current `TwoTierBridge::search()` only uses fast embeddings; the full fast→quality progressive refinement workflow is stubbed.

## Goals

- Ensure thread-safe single initialization of embedder bridges
- Provide meaningful fallback behavior when quality embedder is unavailable
- Implement full progressive search with streaming/async quality refinement
- Maintain sub-millisecond fast-path latency
- Add comprehensive observability for debugging and monitoring

## Architecture Context

The two-tier system uses:
- **Fast tier**: potion-128M (Model2Vec, ~0ms inference, 256 dims)
- **Quality tier**: MiniLM-L6-v2 (FastEmbed/ONNX, ~128ms, 384 dims)

Both embedders use global `OnceLock` singletons for auto-initialization.

## Files of Interest

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs` - Core index and search
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/auto_init.rs` - Global context
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/model2vec.rs` - Fast embedder
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fastembed.rs` - Quality embedder
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs` - TwoTierBridge integration

## Success Criteria

- Zero duplicate bridge creation under concurrent access
- Quality-unavailable documents still participate in semantic search meaningfully
- Progressive refinement yields improved relevance with transparent latency
- All tests pass with -D warnings clippy","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBarn","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:27.353873587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:14:03.613910302Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","search","semantic","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:27.353873587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":514,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started work on child br-2tnl.5.10.2 (forced in_progress due blocked parent-child constraints). Implementing quality-embedding fallback hardening and keeping validations attached to that child.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:07:14Z"},{"id":523,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OVERALL SUCCESS CRITERIA CHECKLIST (aggregate of child beads):

## Core Implementation (P0)
- [ ] T5.10.1: Race condition fixed - only one bridge created under concurrent access
- [ ] T5.10.2: Quality fallback - has_quality flag implemented, no zero-vectors stored
- [ ] T5.10.3: Progressive refinement - search yields Initial then Refined phases

## Testing (P1)
- [ ] T5.10.4: 7+ unit tests for thread-safe init (including 100-thread stress test)
- [ ] T5.10.5: 8+ unit tests for quality fallback (including boundary tests)
- [ ] T5.10.6: 12+ unit tests for progressive refinement (including timeout, edge cases)
- [ ] T5.10.7: E2E script passes all 7 test functions
- [ ] T5.10.10: Benchmarks pass performance targets (<5ms fast search for 10K docs)

## Observability (P1)
- [ ] T5.10.8: All 4 metrics structs implemented
- [ ] T5.10.8: All 8 tracing spans emitted
- [ ] T5.10.8: User-friendly messaging for degraded mode

## Optimization (P2)
- [ ] T5.10.9: Baseline established before optimization
- [ ] T5.10.9: At least one optimization phase completed with measurable improvement

## User Experience
- [ ] Search results include was_refined and explain fields
- [ ] Health check reports two-tier status with install hints if degraded
- [ ] TUI/CLI shows quality coverage stats
- [ ] Configuration via env vars (TWO_TIER_FAST_ONLY, TWO_TIER_QUALITY_TIMEOUT_MS, etc.)

## Definition of Done
All P0 and P1 items complete. P2 items can be deferred to follow-up.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:30:05Z"},{"id":524,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL GAPS IDENTIFIED FROM CODE REVIEW:

1. **INDEX PERSISTENCE**: Current implementation is in-memory only. On restart, all embeddings are lost and must be recomputed. Consider:
   - Persist index to disk (memmap or sqlite blob)
   - Or accept recompute-on-restart for MVP

2. **INDEX SIZE LIMITS**: No bounds on index growth. For large projects:
   - Add configurable MAX_INDEX_DOCS (default: 100K)
   - Implement LRU eviction for oldest/least-accessed docs
   - Add metric for index memory usage

3. **DOCUMENT UPDATES/REMOVAL**: No API to update or remove docs from index:
   - add_document() always appends
   - Need remove_document(doc_id) for deleted messages
   - Need update_document() or remove+add

4. **LARGE DOCUMENT HANDLING**: Embedding very long texts:
   - Model2Vec has implicit token limit (~512 tokens)
   - FastEmbed truncates at 256 tokens by default
   - Add MAX_EMBED_TEXT_LENGTH config (default: 4096 chars)
   - Truncate or chunk long documents

5. **EMBEDDER HEALTH DURING SESSION**: Code at line 806 checks is_available() but:
   - What if embedder crashes mid-session?
   - Add periodic health check and recovery
   - Log warning if embedder becomes unavailable

6. **LEGACY FALLBACK TESTING**: Line 815 falls back to SemanticBridge:
   - Ensure T5.10.7 E2E tests this fallback path
   - Test: disable two-tier, verify legacy semantic still works","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:32:25Z"},{"id":691,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"AzureBarn","text":"Claimed and started. I am implementing aggregate hardening where possible in this parent bead while coordinating with owners of br-2tnl.5.1 and br-2tnl.5.10.2. Immediate focus: assess current code state, close obvious gaps in progressive/fallback wiring, and land tests/diagnostics incrementally.","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:14:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","title":"T5.10.1: Eliminate lazy initialization race in TwoTierBridge","description":"## Problem Statement

The current `try_two_tier_search` function (search_service.rs:776-793) has a race condition:

```rust
fn try_two_tier_search(query: &SearchQuery, limit: usize) -> Option<Vec<SearchResult>> {
    let bridge = get_two_tier_bridge().or_else(|| {
        let _ = init_two_tier_bridge();  // ← Race: multiple threads may call this
        get_two_tier_bridge()
    });
    // ...
}
```

When two threads call `try_two_tier_search` before initialization:
1. Both threads see `get_two_tier_bridge()` return `None`
2. Both threads call `init_two_tier_bridge()`
3. Both create a `TwoTierBridge` (expensive: loads embedders, allocates index)
4. `OnceLock::set` silently drops the second bridge
5. Duplicate "Two-tier semantic bridge initialized" log messages appear

## Impact

- **Resource waste**: Creating TwoTierBridge is expensive (embedder initialization)
- **Confusing logs**: Duplicate initialization messages
- **Theoretical correctness**: Both threads still get a valid bridge, but wasteful

## Solution: OnceLock::get_or_init Pattern

Replace the two-step "check then init" pattern with atomic `get_or_init`:

```rust
static TWO_TIER_BRIDGE: OnceLock<Arc<TwoTierBridge>> = OnceLock::new();

fn get_or_init_two_tier_bridge() -> &'static Arc<TwoTierBridge> {
    TWO_TIER_BRIDGE.get_or_init(|| {
        Arc::new(TwoTierBridge::new())
    })
}
```

However, `TwoTierBridge::new()` may fail if embedders are unavailable. Need to handle:
- `OnceLock<Option<Arc<TwoTierBridge>>>` for fallible init
- Or separate `OnceLock` for availability check

## Implementation Plan

1. Change `TWO_TIER_BRIDGE` to use `get_or_init` pattern
2. Handle fallible initialization gracefully
3. Remove `init_two_tier_bridge()` public API (no longer needed)
4. Update `try_two_tier_search` to use atomic getter
5. Add debug logging for initialization timing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Only one TwoTierBridge created under concurrent access
- [ ] Single "Two-tier semantic bridge initialized" log message
- [ ] No change to search behavior or latency
- [ ] Existing tests pass

## Test Plan

1. Unit test: concurrent access to `get_or_init_two_tier_bridge` from 10 threads
2. Verify only one initialization log message
3. Verify all threads get same Arc pointer (pointer equality)

## Files to Modify

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`
  - `TWO_TIER_BRIDGE` static
  - `init_two_tier_bridge()` → deprecate/remove
  - `get_two_tier_bridge()` → use `get_or_init`
  - `try_two_tier_search()` → simplify","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:46.402926417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:51:39.726160272Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T01:51:39.726140074Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified in search_service.rs (atomic OnceLock get_or_init + concurrency tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","init","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:46.402926417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":490,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: The OnceLock pattern change should be backwards-compatible. Existing code calling init_two_tier_bridge() can be deprecated with #[deprecated] attribute, pointing to get_or_init_two_tier_bridge(). The test for this should verify Arc pointer equality across threads, not just functional correctness.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:43:22Z"},{"id":508,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (CopperTower): Fixed race condition using OnceLock::get_or_init pattern. Added get_or_init_two_tier_bridge() for atomic TwoTierBridge init. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:54:40Z"},{"id":516,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh-eyes audit follow-up: get_or_init_two_tier_bridge_is_thread_safe test now asserts full completion of all 10 spawned threads before checking Option/pointer invariants, preventing partial-result false positives.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:14:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.10","title":"T5.10.10: Benchmark suite for two-tier search performance","description":"## Objective

Comprehensive benchmark suite to validate two-tier search performance targets and prevent regressions.

## Background: Performance Requirements

From the bake-off and production requirements:
- **Fast embedding**: <1ms for any query
- **Fast search (1K docs)**: <1ms
- **Fast search (10K docs)**: <5ms
- **Fast search (100K docs)**: <50ms
- **Quality refinement**: <200ms additional
- **Memory**: <1KB per document (fast + quality embeddings)

## Benchmark Categories

### 1. Micro-benchmarks (per-operation)

\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_dot_product_f16_256dim(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedding: Vec<f16> = (0..256).map(|i| f16::from_f32(i as f32 * 0.01)).collect();
    let query: Vec<f32> = (0..256).map(|i| i as f32 * 0.01).collect();
    
    b.iter(|| {
        black_box(dot_product_f16_simd(black_box(&embedding), black_box(&query)))
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_l2_normalize_256dim(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let mut vec: Vec<f32> = (0..256).map(|i| i as f32 * 0.01).collect();
    b.iter(|| {
        l2_normalize(black_box(&mut vec.clone()));
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_f16_to_f32_conversion_256(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedding: Vec<f16> = (0..256).map(|i| f16::from_f32(i as f32 * 0.01)).collect();
    b.iter(|| {
        let _: Vec<f32> = black_box(&embedding).iter().map(|&x| f32::from(x)).collect();
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Index benchmarks (search operations)

\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_100_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(100);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    b.iter(|| black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10)));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_1000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(1000);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    b.iter(|| black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10)));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_10000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(10000);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    b.iter(|| black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10)));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_progressive_1000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(1000);
    let searcher = create_test_searcher(&index);
    let query = "test query for progressive search";
    
    b.iter(|| {
        let phases: Vec<_> = searcher.search(black_box(query), 10).collect();
        black_box(phases)
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Embedding benchmarks

\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_fast_embedding_short_text(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedder = get_fast_embedder().expect("fast embedder required");
    let text = "short query";
    b.iter(|| black_box(embedder.embed(black_box(text)).unwrap()));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_fast_embedding_long_text(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedder = get_fast_embedder().expect("fast embedder required");
    let text = "This is a much longer piece of text that simulates a typical message body with multiple sentences and various technical terms that might appear in agent mail communications.";
    b.iter(|| black_box(embedder.embed(black_box(text)).unwrap()));
}

#[bench]
#[ignore = "requires quality embedder"]
fn bench_quality_embedding(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedder = get_quality_embedder().expect("quality embedder required");
    let text = "test query for quality embedding";
    b.iter(|| black_box(embedder.embed(black_box(text)).unwrap()));
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Memory benchmarks

\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn test_memory_per_document() {
    let config = TwoTierConfig::default();
    let mut index = TwoTierIndex::new(&config);
    
    // Baseline memory
    let baseline = get_memory_usage();
    
    // Add 1000 documents
    for i in 0..1000 {
        index.add_entry(make_test_entry(i)).unwrap();
    }
    
    let after = get_memory_usage();
    let per_doc = (after - baseline) / 1000;
    
    println!("Memory per document: {} bytes", per_doc);
    assert!(per_doc < 1024, "should be <1KB per doc, was {} bytes", per_doc);
}
\`\`\`

## Benchmark Script: scripts/bench_two_tier.sh

\`\`\`bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Two-Tier Search Performance Benchmark Suite
# Runs all benchmarks and generates report

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
RESULTS_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/benchmark_results/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"

mkdir -p "$RESULTS_DIR"

echo "=== Two-Tier Search Benchmark Suite ==="
echo "Results directory: $RESULTS_DIR"
echo ""

# Run Criterion benchmarks
echo "Running Criterion benchmarks..."
cargo bench --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core \
    --bench two_tier_bench \
    -- --save-baseline current 2>&1 | tee "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log"

# Extract key metrics
echo ""
echo "=== Key Performance Metrics ==="

# Parse Criterion output for key benchmarks
grep -E "bench_search_fast|bench_dot_product|bench_.*_embedding" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | \
    grep -E "time:.*\[" || true

# Memory analysis
echo ""
echo "Running memory analysis..."
cargo test --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core \
    test_memory_per_document \
    -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$RESULTS_DIR/memory.log"

# Generate summary JSON
cat > "$RESULTS_DIR/summary.json" << EOF
{
  "timestamp": "$(date -Iseconds)",
  "git_commit": "$(git rev-parse HEAD)",
  "benchmarks": {
    "dot_product_256dim_ns": $(grep "bench_dot_product_f16_256dim" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+' | head -1 || echo "null"),
    "search_fast_1k_us": $(grep "bench_search_fast_1000" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+' | head -1 || echo "null"),
    "search_fast_10k_us": $(grep "bench_search_fast_10000" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+' | head -1 || echo "null"),
    "memory_per_doc_bytes": $(grep "Memory per document" "$RESULTS_DIR/memory.log" | grep -oP '\d+' | head -1 || echo "null")
  },
  "targets": {
    "search_fast_1k_us": 1000,
    "search_fast_10k_us": 5000,
    "memory_per_doc_bytes": 1024
  }
}
EOF

echo ""
echo "Summary written to: $RESULTS_DIR/summary.json"
cat "$RESULTS_DIR/summary.json"
\`\`\`

## CI Integration

Add to .github/workflows/bench.yml:

\`\`\`yaml
name: Benchmarks
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/**'

jobs:
  benchmark:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core -- --save-baseline pr
      - name: Compare with main
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          cargo bench --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core -- --baseline main --save-baseline pr
\`\`\`

## Logging and Diagnostics

Each benchmark run should emit:

\`\`\`
[BENCH] two_tier.dot_product_256: 45ns ± 2ns (1000 iterations)
[BENCH] two_tier.search_fast_1k: 892µs ± 34µs (100 iterations)
[BENCH] two_tier.search_progressive_1k: 145ms ± 12ms (10 iterations)
  └─ [PHASE] initial: 1.2ms
  └─ [PHASE] refinement: 143.8ms
[BENCH] two_tier.memory_per_doc: 756 bytes
[CHECK] ✓ All performance targets met
\`\`\`

## Performance Regression Detection

Fail CI if:
- Fast search >2x slower than baseline
- Memory per doc >1.5x larger than target
- Progressive refinement >300ms

## Files to Create

- \`crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/benches/two_tier_bench.rs\` - Criterion benchmarks
- \`scripts/bench_two_tier.sh\` - Benchmark runner script
- \`.github/workflows/bench.yml\` - CI integration (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All micro-benchmarks implemented with Criterion
- [ ] Index benchmarks cover 100, 1K, 10K doc sizes
- [ ] Memory benchmark validates <1KB/doc target
- [ ] Benchmark script produces structured JSON output
- [ ] Detailed logging shows per-phase timings

1. WARM-UP: All benchmarks must include warm-up iterations to ensure:
   - CPU frequency scaling has stabilized
   - Caches are populated
   - JIT/branch prediction is trained
   - Criterion handles this automatically, but verify with --warm-up-time 3

2. ENVIRONMENT CONTROL:
   - Document CPU model, memory, OS version in results
   - Pin process to specific CPU cores: taskset -c 0,1 cargo bench
   - Disable CPU frequency scaling during benchmarks: cpupower frequency-set -g performance
   - Close background applications

3. NOISE HANDLING:
   - Run each benchmark multiple times (Criterion default: 100 samples)
   - Report median and IQR, not just mean
   - Use Criterion's change detection: cargo bench -- --baseline main
   - Flag any result with >10% variance for manual review

4. DETERMINISTIC SEED:
   - Use fixed random seed for test data generation:
```rust
fn build_test_index(size: usize) -> TwoTierIndex {
    let mut rng = StdRng::seed_from_u64(42);  // Fixed seed for reproducibility
    // ...
}
```

5. REGRESSION DETECTION THRESHOLDS:
   - P50 latency regression >20%: WARNING
   - P95 latency regression >30%: WARNING
   - Any metric regression >50%: FAIL CI
   - Memory per doc increase >25%: FAIL CI","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:29:03Z"}]}
